questions.question: What is the primary function of a router?
options: To connect devices within a single local area network (LAN).
To filter network traffic based on MAC addresses.
To forward data packets between different computer networks.
To provide wireless connectivity to devices.
questions.correct_answer: To forward data packets between different computer networks.

questions.question: Which OSI layer is responsible for converting data packets into frames and handling physical addressing (MAC addresses)?
What protocol is primarily used by web browsers to request and display web pages?
Which of the following is a connection-oriented protocol that guarantees reliable delivery of data?
What is the main purpose of the Domain Name System (DNS)?
options: To assign IP addresses to devices automatically.
To encrypt data transmitted over the internet.
To translate human-readable domain names into IP addresses.
questions.correct_answer: To translate human-readable domain names into IP addresses.

questions.question: An IP address in the range 192.168.0.0 to 192.168.255.255 typically belongs to which type of network?
What networking device operates at Layer 2 (Data Link Layer) of the OSI model and forwards frames based on MAC addresses?
Port number 443 is commonly associated with which secure communication protocol?
Which protocol is used to dynamically assign IP addresses to client devices on a network?
What is the purpose of the Address Resolution Protocol (ARP)?
options: To translate domain names to IP addresses.
To resolve an IP address to a MAC address.
To establish a secure connection between two devices.
To provide error reporting for IP packet delivery.
questions.correct_answer: To resolve an IP address to a MAC address.

questions.question: The subnet mask is used to determine which portion of an IP address represents the network ID and which represents the host ID. True or False?
Which device acts as a barrier between a trusted internal network and an untrusted external network, controlling incoming and outgoing network traffic?
How many bits are in an IPv6 address?
What does VPN stand for?
In a star network topology, all devices are connected to a central device. What is this central device typically called?
What is the maximum theoretical speed for a Fast Ethernet connection?
Which network type spans a large geographical area, often connecting multiple smaller networks (like LANs)?
What is the primary difference between a hub and a switch?
options: A hub filters traffic, while a switch broadcasts all traffic.
A hub works at Layer 3, while a switch works at Layer 2.
A hub broadcasts data to all connected devices, while a switch forwards data only to the intended recipient.
A hub is faster than a switch.
questions.correct_answer: A hub broadcasts data to all connected devices, while a switch forwards data only to the intended recipient.

questions.question: What is a MAC address used for?
options: Identifying a computer's location on the internet.
Logically identifying a device on a network (Layer 3).
Physically identifying a network interface card (NIC) on a local network (Layer 2).
questions.correct_answer: Physically identifying a network interface card (NIC) on a local network (Layer 2).

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a layer in the TCP/IP model?
